text: Measured from the c1 scoreboard: training is the expensive lever (~867s/agent-train-day, batched ~10×).
Eval is cheap (~70s/agent-eval-day, serial); a uniform per-agent-day average blended the two and led to cutting the cheap thing (eval days) while maxing the expensive thing (train days).

locator.quote: 2 eval days starved the selection metric.

text: Cutting to 2 eval days starved the selection metric: locked_per_std's σ over n=2 is meaningless, giving the GA no clean gradient — match eval-day count to the metric's variance needs before launch.
